title: Explain deadlock in Java. How can it be prevented?

answers.junior: Deadlock is when two or more threads wait forever for each other to release resources.
answers.middle: Deadlock occurs when threads hold resources and wait for others, causing a standstill. Prevent by avoiding nested locks and using lock ordering.
answers.senior: Deadlock is a cyclic dependency of locks. Prevent by acquiring locks in a consistent order, using tryLock(), and minimizing lock scope. Analyze with thread dumps.
answers.tricky: Use java.util.concurrent tools to avoid deadlocks. Apply lock timeouts, avoid unnecessary synchronization, and design for lock-free concurrency. Monitor and analyze thread states in production.
